architecture.summary: VRPRM is a multimodal process reward model (PRM) that augments visual reasoning with chain-of-thought (CoT) capability. It uses a two-stage training pipeline: (1) Cold-start SFT with 3.6K high-quality CoT-PRM data (synthesized via Claude-3.7-Sonnet prompting) to activate reasoning and process evaluation; (2) RL scaling with 50K non-CoT PRM data using GRPO to reinforce step-level accuracy. The model reads image + problem + solution, generates explicit reasoning chains within <think></think> tokens, evaluates each solution step, and outputs structured JSON with per-step correctness scores. At inference, it predicts the probability of token '1' as the reward for each step, enabling best-of-N decoding by selecting the highest-scoring response.
trained_components: LoRA adapters for CoT reasoning and process evaluation (cold-start SFT)
full model weights via GRPO RL on step-level PRM data

biblio.title: VRPRM: Process Reward Modeling via Visual Reasoning

claims.dataset: VisualProcessBench (average across MMMU, MathVision, MathVerse-VO, DynaMath, WeMath)
claims.statement: VRPRM surpasses non-thinking PRM with 400K training data while using only 3.6K CoT-PRM SFT data and 50K non-CoT PRM RL training data (total 53.6K)

claims.dataset: multimodal reasoning benchmarks (MathVista, MathVision, MathVerse-VO, WeMath, LogicVista)
claims.statement: VRPRM achieves up to 118% relative performance improvement over the base model in the BoN (Best-of-N) experiment
VRPRM-MiMo achieves average AEI of 66.44 and average FEI of 51.54, outperforming VisualPRM by 5.41 in AEI and 25.93 in FEI
VRPRM w/o RL, trained on only 3.6K samples, achieved average AEI of 62.11, outperforming all other open-source and proprietary models except MiMo-VL-7B
Removing CoT causes Average All Error Identification to drop from 66.00 to 53.66 for VRPRM-Qwen, and First Error Identification to decline from 49.72 to 38.14
RL training on non-CoT PRM data improves VRPRM performance by 3.92% average on VisualProcessBench, with gains across all sub-datasets
CoT reasoning increases average output tokens per sample from 10.03 to 339.73 and processing time from 0.39 to 16.94 seconds per sample
VRPRM provides transparent reasoning processes, transforming the model from a scorer to a white-box trustworthy verifier
